message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

body:
## рџЋЇ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager` package for the newly added `ArmClientHostExtensions` class, which provides `AddArmClient` / `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods for registering `ArmClient` in a .NET Generic Host DI container. The CHANGELOG, README, and sample code need to be updated to reflect these additions.

<details>
<summary>рџ“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager` (1.15.0-beta.1, Unreleased)
**Affected API:** `ArmClientHostExtensions` вЂ” `AddArmClient`, `AddKeyedArmClient` (experimental, `SCME0002`)
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` has an empty `Features Added` section despite the new extension methods being merged in PR #58123
- README.md has no `IHostApplicationBuilder` / DI registration guidance
- No sample/test snippet demonstrates the new `AddArmClient` usage in a host startup context

**Root ask:** Add CHANGELOG entry, a README "Use with dependency injection" subsection, and a sample code file (`tests/Samples/Sample_HostedService.cs`) showing `AddArmClient` usage.
</details>

<details>
<summary>рџ”Ћ Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The new code was introduced by commit `a40c877d8decd79accea92d741637f7ec88211dd` (PR #58123). The extensions live in `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Extensions/ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify `ArmClientHostExtensions.cs` exists and the four methods are present after the referenced commit
2. Update `CHANGELOG.md` under `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` в†’ `### Features Added` with a description of the new extension methods
3. Add a `### Use with dependency injection` subsection to `README.md` under `## Getting started`, after `### Authenticate the Client`
4. Create `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_HostedService.cs` with the `ResourceManager_AddArmClient` snippet, then run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcemanager` to inject it into the README
5. Build and run tests: `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live`
</details>

<details>
<summary>рџЏ·пёЏ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` вЂ” the affected package is `Azure.ResourceManager`, which is a management-plane SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.*` naming)
- **Service:** `ARM` вЂ” `Azure.ResourceManager` (core) maps to the ARM service label; confirmed by reference issues [#49396](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/49396) and [#44466](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/44466) which have `ARM` + `Mgmt` labels and meet quality criteria
- **Confidence:** High вЂ” the package is explicitly named, unambiguous category and service, consistent with reference issue patterns
</details>

<details>
<summary>рџ‘Ґ Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %ARM %Mgmt` (line 918) вЂ” both predicted labels (ARM + Mgmt) are present
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed for this entry
- **ServiceOwners:** @Azure/armleads-azure
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners only, no AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan from line 1228; encountered line 918 (`%ARM %Mgmt` в†’ @armleads-azure) before line 915 (`%ARM %Mgmt` в†’ @Azure/arm-sdk-owners); line 918 matched first and scan stopped; `%Mgmt` catch-all at line 903 was not reached
</details>
